description: Add an existing insight to a dashboard. Requires insight ID and dashboard ID. Optionally supports layout and color customization.
name: add-insight-to-dashboard
title: Add insight to dashboard

description: Create a new dashboard in the project. Requires name and optional description, tags, and other properties.
name: dashboard-create
title: Create dashboard

description: Delete a dashboard by ID (soft delete - marks as deleted).
name: dashboard-delete
title: Delete dashboard

description: Get a specific dashboard by ID. The response will include insights / tiles that are on the dashboard.
name: dashboard-get
title: Get dashboard

description: Get all dashboards in the project with optional filtering. Can filter by pinned status, search term, or pagination.
name: dashboards-get-all
title: Get all dashboards

description: Update an existing dashboard by ID. Can update name, description, pinned status or tags.
name: dashboard-update
title: Update dashboard

description: Reorder tiles (insights) on a dashboard. Provide an array of tile IDs in the desired order from top to bottom. First, use dashboard-get to retrieve the dashboard and see the current tile IDs, then provide the tile IDs in your desired order.

dashboardId.description: The ID of the dashboard to reorder tiles on

tileOrder.description: Array of tile IDs in the desired order from top to bottom

name: dashboard-reorder-tiles
title: Reorder dashboard tiles

description: Use this tool to search the PostHog documentation for information that can help the user with their request. Use it as a fallback when you cannot answer the user's request using other tools in this MCP. Only use this tool for PostHog related questions.
name: docs-search
title: Search docs

description: Use this tool to get the details of an error in the project.
name: error-details
title: Get error details

description: Use this tool to list errors in the project.
name: list-errors
title: List errors

description: Use this tool to update the status of an error tracking issue. Valid statuses are: active, resolved, archived, suppressed, or pending_release.

issueId.description: The ID of the error tracking issue to update

status.description: The new status for the issue: active, resolved, archived, suppressed, or pending_release

name: update-issue-status
title: Update issue status

description: Creates a new feature flag in the project. Once you have created a feature flag, you should: Ask the user if they want to add it to their codebase, Use the "search-docs" tool to find documentation on how to add feature flags to the codebase (search for the right language / framework), Clarify where it should be added and then add it.

variant.description: Variant key to serve for this condition (for multivariate flags)

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users who will see this variant (0-100). Must be an integer.

name: create-feature-flag
title: Create feature flag

description: Delete a feature flag in the project.
name: delete-feature-flag
title: Delete feature flag

description: Get all feature flags in the project.
name: feature-flag-get-all
title: Get all feature flags

description: Get the definition of a feature flag. You can provide either the flagId or the flagKey. If you provide both, the flagId will be used.
name: feature-flag-get-definition
title: Get feature flag definition

description: Update a new feature flag in the project. To enable a feature flag, you should make sure it is active and the rollout percentage is set to 100 for the group you want to target. To disable a feature flag, you should make sure it is inactive, you can keep the rollout percentage as it is.

variant.description: Variant key to serve for this condition (for multivariate flags)

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users who will see this variant (0-100). Must be an integer.

name: update-feature-flag
title: Update feature flag

description: Get all experiments in the project.
name: experiment-get-all
title: Get all experiments

description: Create a comprehensive A/B test experiment. PROCESS: 1) Understand experiment goal and hypothesis 2) Search existing feature flags with 'feature-flags-get-all' tool first and suggest reuse or new key 3) Help user define success metrics by asking what they want to optimize 4) MOST IMPORTANT: Use 'event-definitions-list' tool to find available events in their project 5) For funnel metrics, ask for specific event sequence (e.g., ['product_view', 'add_to_cart', 'purchase']) and use funnel_steps parameter 6) Configure variants (default 50/50 control/test unless they specify otherwise) 7) Set targeting criteria if needed.

description.description: Detailed description of the experiment hypothesis, what changes are being tested, and expected outcomes

draft.description: Create as draft (true) or launch immediately (false). Recommend draft for review first

feature_flag_key.description: Feature flag key (letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores only). IMPORTANT: First search for existing feature flags that might be suitable using the feature-flags-get-all tool, then suggest reusing existing ones or creating a new key based on the experiment name

filter_test_accounts.description: Whether to filter out internal test accounts

holdout_id.description: Holdout group ID if this experiment should exclude users from other experiments

minimum_detectable_effect.description: Minimum detectable effect in percentage. Lower values require more users but detect smaller changes. Suggest 20-30% for most experiments

name.description: Experiment name - should clearly describe what is being tested

primary_metrics.description: Primary metrics to measure experiment success. IMPORTANT: Each metric needs event_name to track data. For funnels, provide funnel_steps array with event names for each step. Ask user what events they track, or use project-property-definitions to find available events.

description.description: What this metric measures and why it's important for the experiment

event_name.description: REQUIRED for metrics to work: PostHog event name (e.g., '$pageview', 'add_to_cart', 'purchase'). For funnels, this is the first step. Use '$pageview' if unsure. Search project-property-definitions tool for available events.

funnel_steps.description: For funnel metrics only: Array of event names for each funnel step (e.g., ['product_view', 'add_to_cart', 'checkout', 'purchase'])

metric_type.description: Metric type: 'mean' for average values (revenue, time spent), 'funnel' for conversion flows, 'ratio' for comparing two metrics

properties.description: Event property filters as an array, e.g. [{ key: "$browser", value: "Chrome", operator: "exact", type: "event" }]

secondary_metrics.description: Secondary metrics to monitor for potential side effects or additional insights. Each metric needs event_name.

event_name.description: REQUIRED: PostHog event name. Use '$pageview' if unsure.

funnel_steps.description: For funnel metrics only: Array of event names for each funnel step

metric_type.description: Metric type: 'mean' for average values, 'funnel' for conversion flows, 'ratio' for comparing two metrics

properties.description: Event property filters as an array, e.g. [{ key: "$browser", value: "Chrome", operator: "exact", type: "event" }]

target_properties.description: Properties to target specific user segments (e.g., country, subscription type)

variants.description: Experiment variants. If not specified, defaults to 50/50 control/test split. Ask user how many variants they need and what each tests

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users to show this variant

name: experiment-create
title: Create experiment

description: Delete an experiment by ID.

experimentId.description: The ID of the experiment to delete

name: experiment-delete
title: Delete experiment

description: Update an existing experiment by ID. Can update name, description, lifecycle state, variants, metrics, and other properties. RESTART WORKFLOW: To restart a concluded experiment, set end_date=null, conclusion=null, conclusion_comment=null, and optionally set a new start_date. To make it draft again, also set start_date=null. COMMON PATTERNS: Launch draft (set start_date), stop running (set end_date + conclusion), archive (set archived=true), modify variants (update parameters.feature_flag_variants). NOTE: feature_flag_key cannot be changed after creation.

data.description: The experiment data to update using user-friendly format

launch.description: Launch experiment (set start_date) or keep as draft

minimum_detectable_effect.description: Update minimum detectable effect in percentage

funnel_steps.description: For funnel metrics only: Array of event names for each funnel step

metric_type.description: Metric type: 'mean' for average values, 'funnel' for conversion flows, 'ratio' for comparing two metrics

properties.description: Event property filters as an array, e.g. [{ key: "$browser", value: "Chrome", operator: "exact", type: "event" }]

restart.description: Restart concluded experiment (clears end_date and conclusion)

funnel_steps.description: For funnel metrics only: Array of event names

properties.description: Event property filters as an array, e.g. [{ key: "$browser", value: "Chrome", operator: "exact", type: "event" }]

experimentId.description: The ID of the experiment to update

name: experiment-update
title: Update experiment

description: Get details of a specific experiment by ID.

experimentId.description: The ID of the experiment to retrieve

name: experiment-get
title: Get experiment details

description: Get comprehensive experiment results including all metrics data (primary and secondary) and exposure data. This tool fetches the experiment details and executes the necessary queries to get complete experiment results. Only works with new experiments (not legacy experiments).

experimentId.description: The ID of the experiment to get comprehensive results for

refresh.description: Force refresh of results instead of using cached values

name: experiment-results-get
title: Get experiment results

description: Create an insight from a query that you have previously tested with 'query-run'. You should check the query runs, before creating an insight. Do not create an insight before running the query, unless you know already that it is correct (e.g. you are making a minor modification to an existing query you have seen).

source.description: For new insights, use the query from your successful query-run tool call. For updates, the existing query can optionally be reused.

name: insight-create-from-query
title: Create insight from query

description: Delete an insight by ID (soft delete - marks as deleted).
name: insight-delete
title: Delete insight

description: Get a specific insight by ID.
name: insight-get
title: Get insight

description: Execute a query on an existing insight to get its results/data. Provide the insight ID to retrieve the current query results.
name: insight-query
title: Query insight

description: Get all insights in the project with optional filtering. Can filter by saved status, favorited status, or search term.
name: insights-get-all
title: Get all insights

description: Update an existing insight by ID. Can update name, description, filters, and other properties. You should get the insight before update it to see it's current query structure, and only modify the parts needed to answer the user's request.

source.description: For new insights, use the query from your successful query-run tool call. For updates, the existing query can optionally be reused

name: insight-update
title: Update insight

description: You should use this to answer questions that a user has about their data and for when you want to create a new insight. You can use 'event-definitions-list' to get events to use in the query, and 'event-properties-list' to get properties for those events. It can run a trend, funnel or HogQL query. Where possible, use a trend or funnel rather than a HogQL query, unless you know the HogQL is correct (e.g. it came from a previous insight.).
name: query-run
title: Run query

description: This is a slow tool, and you should only use it once you have tried to create a query using the 'query-run' tool, or the query is too complicated to create a trend / funnel. Queries project's PostHog data based on a provided natural language question - don't provide SQL query as input but describe the output you want. When giving the results back to the user, first show the SQL query that was used, then provide results in easily readable format. You should also offer to save the query as an insight if the user wants to.

question.description: Your natural language query describing the SQL insight (max 1000 characters).

name: query-generate-hogql-from-question
title: Generate SQL

description: Fetches the total LLM daily costs for each model for a project over a given number of days. If no number of days is provided, it defaults to 7. The results are sorted by model name. The total cost is rounded to 4 decimal places. The query is executed against the project's data warehouse. Show the results as a Markdown formatted table with the following information for each model: Model name, Total cost in USD, Each day's date, Each day's cost in USD. Write in bold the model name with the highest total cost. Properly render the markdown table in the response.
name: get-llm-total-costs-for-project
title: Get LLM costs

description: Get the details of the active organization.
name: organization-details-get
title: Get organization details

description: Get the organizations the user has access to.
name: organizations-get
title: Get organizations

description: Change the active organization from the default organization. You should only use this tool if the user asks you to change the organization - otherwise, the default organization will be used.
name: switch-organization
title: Switch active organization

description: Fetches projects that the user has access to in the current organization.
name: projects-get
title: Get projects

description: List all event definitions in the project with optional filtering. Can filter by search term.

q.description: Search query to filter event names. Only use if there are lots of events.

name: event-definitions-list
title: List all events

description: Update event definition metadata. Can update description, tags, mark status as verified or hidden. Use exact event name like '$pageview' or 'user_signed_up'.

data.description: The event definition data to update

description.description: Description explaining when the event is triggered

hidden.description: Mark event as no longer used/captured. Hides it from UI while preserving historical data

tags.description: Tags to organize events by product area (e.g. "checkout", "onboarding") or user journey stage (e.g. "acquisition", "activation", "monetization", "retention")

verified.description: Mark as verified to indicate the event is properly instrumented and tracking correctly

eventName.description: The name of the event to update (e.g. "$pageview", "user_signed_up")

name: event-definition-update
title: Update event definition

description: List properties for events or persons. If fetching event properties, you must provide an event name.

eventName.description: Event name to filter properties by, required for event type

name: properties-list
title: Get properties

description: Change the active project from the default project. You should only use this tool if the user asks you to change the project - otherwise, the default project will be used.
name: switch-project
title: Switch active project

description: Creates a new survey in the project. Surveys can be popover or API-based and support various question types including open-ended, multiple choice, rating, and link questions. Once created, you should ask the user if they want to add the survey to their application code.

enable_partial_responses.description: When at least one question is answered, the response is stored (true). The response is stored when all questions are answered (false).

iteration_count.description: For a recurring schedule, this field specifies the number of times the survey should be shown to the user. Use 1 for 'once every X days', higher numbers for multiple repetitions. Works together with iteration_frequency_days to determine the overall survey schedule.

iteration_frequency_days.description: For a recurring schedule, this field specifies the interval in days between each survey instance shown to the user, used alongside iteration_count for precise scheduling.

linked_flag_id.description: The feature flag linked to this survey

anyOf.description: For rating questions: use sentiment keys based on scale thirds - negative (lower third), neutral (middle third), positive (upper third)

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

display.description: Display format: 'number' shows numeric scale, 'emoji' shows emoji scale

lowerBoundLabel.description: Label for the lowest rating (e.g., 'Very Poor')

scale.description: Rating scale can be one of 3, 5, or 7

anyOf.description: For NPS rating questions: use sentiment keys based on score ranges - detractors (0-6), passives (7-8), promoters (9-10)

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

display.description: NPS questions always use numeric scale

lowerBoundLabel.description: Label for 0 rating (typically 'Not at all likely')

anyOf.description: For single choice questions: use choice indices as string keys ("0", "1", "2", etc.)

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

choices.description: Array of choice options. Choice indices (0, 1, 2, etc.) are used for branching logic

hasOpenChoice.description: Whether the last choice (typically 'Other', is an open text input question

shuffleOptions.description: Whether to randomize the order of choices for each respondent

choices.description: Array of choice options. Multiple selections allowed. No branching logic supported.

hasOpenChoice.description: Whether the last choice (typically 'Other', is an open text input question

shuffleOptions.description: Whether to randomize the order of choices for each respondent

responses_limit.description: The maximum number of responses before automatically stopping the survey.

start_date.description: Setting this will launch the survey immediately. Don't add a start_date unless explicitly requested to do so.

targeting_flag_filters.description: Target specific users based on their properties. Example: {groups: [{properties: [{key: 'email', value: ['@company.com'], operator: 'icontains'}], rollout_percentage: 100}]}

variant.description: Variant key to serve for this condition (for multivariate flags)

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users who will see this variant (0-100). Must be an integer.

name: survey-create
title: Create survey

description: Get a specific survey by ID. Returns the survey configuration including questions, targeting, and scheduling details.
name: survey-get
title: Get survey

description: Get all surveys in the project with optional filtering. Can filter by search term or use pagination.
name: surveys-get-all
title: Get all surveys

description: Update an existing survey by ID. Can update name, description, questions, scheduling, and other survey properties.

deviceTypesMatchType.description: URL/device matching types: 'regex' (matches regex pattern), 'not_regex' (does not match regex pattern), 'exact' (exact string match), 'is_not' (not exact match), 'icontains' (case-insensitive contains), 'not_icontains' (case-insensitive does not contain)

repeatedActivation.description: Whether to show the survey every time one of the events is triggered (true), or just once (false)

values.description: Array of event names that trigger the survey

linkedFlagVariant.description: The variant of the feature flag linked to this survey

seenSurveyWaitPeriodInDays.description: Don't show this survey to users who saw any survey in the last x days.

urlMatchType.description: URL/device matching types: 'regex' (matches regex pattern), 'not_regex' (does not match regex pattern), 'exact' (exact string match), 'is_not' (not exact match), 'icontains' (case-insensitive contains), 'not_icontains' (case-insensitive does not contain)

enable_partial_responses.description: When at least one question is answered, the response is stored (true). The response is stored when all questions are answered (false).

end_date.description: When the survey stopped being shown to users. Setting this will complete the survey.

iteration_count.description: For a recurring schedule, this field specifies the number of times the survey should be shown to the user. Use 1 for 'once every X days', higher numbers for multiple repetitions. Works together with iteration_frequency_days to determine the overall survey schedule.

iteration_frequency_days.description: For a recurring schedule, this field specifies the interval in days between each survey instance shown to the user, used alongside iteration_count for precise scheduling.

linked_flag_id.description: The feature flag to link to this survey

anyOf.description: For rating questions: use sentiment keys based on scale thirds - negative (lower third), neutral (middle third), positive (upper third)

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

display.description: Display format: 'number' shows numeric scale, 'emoji' shows emoji scale

lowerBoundLabel.description: Label for the lowest rating (e.g., 'Very Poor')

scale.description: Rating scale can be one of 3, 5, or 7

anyOf.description: For NPS rating questions: use sentiment keys based on score ranges - detractors (0-6), passives (7-8), promoters (9-10)

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

display.description: NPS questions always use numeric scale

lowerBoundLabel.description: Label for 0 rating (typically 'Not at all likely')

anyOf.description: For single choice questions: use choice indices as string keys ("0", "1", "2", etc.)

responseValues.description: Only include keys for responses that should branch to a specific question or 'end'. Omit keys for responses that should proceed to the next question (default behavior).

choices.description: Array of choice options. Choice indices (0, 1, 2, etc.) are used for branching logic

hasOpenChoice.description: Whether the last choice (typically 'Other', is an open text input question

shuffleOptions.description: Whether to randomize the order of choices for each respondent

choices.description: Array of choice options. Multiple selections allowed. No branching logic supported.

hasOpenChoice.description: Whether the last choice (typically 'Other', is an open text input question

shuffleOptions.description: Whether to randomize the order of choices for each respondent

remove_targeting_flag.description: Set to true to completely remove all targeting filters from the survey, making it visible to all users (subject to other display conditions like URL matching).

responses_limit.description: The maximum number of responses before automatically stopping the survey.

schedule.description: Survey scheduling behavior: 'once' = show once per user (default), 'recurring' = repeat based on iteration_count and iteration_frequency_days settings, 'always' = show every time conditions are met (mainly for widget surveys)

start_date.description: When the survey should start being shown to users. Setting this will launch the survey

targeting_flag_filters.description: Target specific users based on their properties. Example: {groups: [{properties: [{key: 'email', value: ['@company.com'], operator: 'icontains'}], rollout_percentage: 50}]}

variant.description: Variant key to serve for this condition (for multivariate flags)

rollout_percentage.description: Percentage of users who will see this variant (0-100). Must be an integer.

targeting_flag_id.description: An existing targeting flag to use for this survey

name: survey-update
title: Update survey

description: Delete a survey by ID (soft delete - marks as archived).
name: survey-delete
title: Delete survey

description: Get aggregated response statistics across all surveys in the project. Includes event counts (shown, dismissed, sent), unique respondents, conversion rates, and timing data. Supports optional date filtering.

date_from.description: Optional ISO timestamp for start date (e.g. 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z)

date_to.description: Optional ISO timestamp for end date (e.g. 2024-01-31T23:59:59Z)

name: surveys-global-stats
title: Get all survey response stats

description: Get response statistics for a specific survey. Includes detailed event counts (shown, dismissed, sent), unique respondents, conversion rates, and timing data. Supports optional date filtering.

date_from.description: Optional ISO timestamp for start date (e.g. 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z)

date_to.description: Optional ISO timestamp for end date (e.g. 2024-01-31T23:59:59Z)

name: survey-stats
title: Get survey response stats

description: Search and query logs in the project. Supports filtering by severity levels (trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal), service names, date range, and free text search. Returns log entries with their attributes, timestamps, and trace information. Supports pagination via cursor.

after.description: Cursor for pagination (from previous response nextCursor)

dateFrom.description: Start of date range (ISO 8601 format, e.g., "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z")

dateTo.description: End of date range (ISO 8601 format, e.g., "2024-01-02T00:00:00Z")

searchTerm.description: Free text search term to filter logs

severityLevels.description: Filter by severity levels (trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)

name: logs-query
title: Query logs

description: List available log attributes in the project. Use this to discover what attributes you can filter on when querying logs. Supports filtering by attribute type (log or resource) and searching by attribute name.

attributeType.description: Type of attributes to list: "log" for log attributes, "resource" for resource attributes (default: log)

name: logs-list-attributes
title: List log attributes

description: Get possible values for a specific log attribute. Use this to discover what values exist for a given attribute key, which helps when building log queries with filters.

attributeType.description: Type of attribute: "log" for log attributes, "resource" for resource attributes (default: log)

key.description: The attribute key to get values for

name: logs-list-attribute-values
title: List attribute values

description: Get all actions in the project. Actions are reusable event definitions that can combine multiple trigger conditions (page views, clicks, form submissions) into a single trackable event for use in insights and funnels. Supports pagination with limit and offset parameters. Note: Search/filtering by name is not supported on this endpoint.

limit.description: Maximum number of actions to return

offset.description: Number of actions to skip for pagination

name: actions-get-all
title: Get all actions

description: Get a specific action by ID. Returns the action configuration including all steps and their trigger conditions.

actionId.description: The ID of the action to retrieve

name: action-get
title: Get action

description: Create a new action in the project. Actions define reusable event triggers based on page views, clicks, form submissions, or custom events. Each action can have multiple steps (OR conditions). Use actions to create composite events for insights and funnels. Example: Create a 'Sign Up Click' action with steps matching button clicks on the signup page.

description.description: Description of what this action represents

name.description: Name of the action (must be unique within the project)

post_to_slack.description: Whether to post to Slack when this action is triggered

steps.description: Action steps - each step defines a trigger condition. Multiple steps are OR-ed together.

event.description: Event name (e.g., '$pageview', '$autocapture', or custom event name)

name: action-create
title: Create action

description: Update an existing action by ID. Can update name, description, steps, tags, and Slack notification settings.

actionId.description: The ID of the action to update

pinned_at.description: Pin timestamp (set to pin, null to unpin)

event.description: Event name (e.g., '$pageview', '$autocapture', or custom event name)

name: action-update
title: Update action

description: Delete an action by ID (soft delete - marks as deleted). The action will no longer appear in lists but historical data is preserved.

actionId.description: The ID of the action to delete

name: action-delete
title: Delete action

description: Search for PostHog entities by name or description. Can search across multiple entity types including insights, dashboards, experiments, feature flags, notebooks, actions, cohorts, event definitions, and surveys. Use this to find entities when you know part of their name. Returns matching entities with their IDs and URLs.

entities.description: Entity types to search. If not specified, searches all types. Available: insight, dashboard, experiment, feature_flag, notebook, action, cohort, event_definition, survey

query.description: Search query to find entities by name or description

name: entity-search
title: Search entities

description: Demo tool for testing MCP Apps SDK integration. Returns sample data that is displayed in an interactive UI app. Use this to verify that MCP Apps are working correctly.

message.description: Optional message to include in the demo data

name: demo-mcp-ui-apps
title: Demo MCP UI Apps

description: List all cohorts in the project. Returns both static cohorts (manually defined user lists) and dynamic cohorts (automatically updated based on property filters).

limit.description: Number of results to return per page.

offset.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

name: cohorts-list
title: List all cohorts

description: Create a new cohort. For dynamic cohorts, provide 'filters' with AND/OR groups of property conditions (person properties, behavioral filters, or cohort references). For static cohorts, set 'is_static: true' then use the 'cohorts-add-persons-to-static-cohort-partial-update' tool to add person UUIDs.

cohort_type.description:
Type of cohort based on filter complexity

* `static` - static
* `person_property` - person_property
* `behavioral` - behavioral
* `realtime` - realtime
* `analytical` - analytical

properties.description: AND/OR group containing cohort filters. Named to avoid collision with analytics Group model.

name: cohorts-create
title: Create cohort

description: Get a specific cohort by ID. Returns the cohort name, description, filters (for dynamic cohorts), count of matching users, and calculation status.

id.description: A unique integer value identifying this cohort.

name: cohorts-retrieve
title: Get cohort

description: Update an existing cohort's name, description, or filters. Changing filters on a dynamic cohort triggers recalculation. To soft-delete a cohort, set 'deleted: true'.

cohort_type.description:
Type of cohort based on filter complexity

* `static` - static
* `person_property` - person_property
* `behavioral` - behavioral
* `realtime` - realtime
* `analytical` - analytical

properties.description: AND/OR group containing cohort filters. Named to avoid collision with analytics Group model.

id.description: A unique integer value identifying this cohort.

name: cohorts-partial-update
title: Update cohort

description: Add persons to a static cohort by their UUIDs. Only works for static cohorts (is_static: true).

id.description: A unique integer value identifying this cohort.

person_ids.description: List of person UUIDs to add to the cohort

name: cohorts-add-persons-to-static-cohort-partial-update
title: Add persons to static cohort

description: Remove a person from a static cohort by their UUID. Only works for static cohorts (is_static: true). The person must exist in the project. Idempotent: removing a person who exists but is not a member of the cohort succeeds silently.

id.description: A unique integer value identifying this cohort.

person_id.description: Person UUID to remove from the cohort

name: cohorts-remove-person-from-static-cohort-partial-update
title: Remove person from static cohort

description: List all error tracking issues in the project. Returns issues with status, volume, first/last seen timestamps, and assignee info.

limit.description: Number of results to return per page.

offset.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

name: error-tracking-issues-list
title: List error tracking issues

description: Get a specific error tracking issue by ID. Returns full issue details including status, description, volume, and metadata.

id.description: A UUID string identifying this error tracking issue.

name: error-tracking-issues-retrieve
title: Get error tracking issue

description: Update an error tracking issue. Can change status (active, resolved, suppressed), assign to a user, or update description.

id.description: A UUID string identifying this error tracking issue.

status.description:
* `archived` - Archived
* `active` - Active
* `resolved` - Resolved
* `pending_release` - Pending release
* `suppressed` - Suppressed

title: Update error tracking issue

description: List all workflows in the project. Returns workflows with their name, description, status (draft/active/archived), version, trigger configuration, and timestamps.

limit.description: Number of results to return per page.

offset.description: The initial index from which to return the results.

name: workflows-list
title: List workflows

description: Get a specific workflow by ID. Returns the full workflow definition including trigger, edges, actions, exit condition, and variables.

id.description: A UUID string identifying this hog flow.

name: workflows-get
title: Get workflow
